title: Steel Can Be Cut
description: On pages 9–12, this part focuses on steel can be cut, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: Steel Can Be Cut. Zoro pushes past his limits against Mr. 1 and discovers the state of mind needed to cut steel. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Fufu... I bet a wool sweater could create more electricity than this.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 1
dialogue.text: You can't be sure until you've tried it!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: According to usopp, I have an ultimate attack to use as a Trump card! So what I have to do...

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 1
dialogue.text: Is create an opening so the attack won't miss!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: The question is how to create that opening,

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 1
dialogue.text: Using the 3 types of bubbles and the hot, acid climate of this country!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: If I only had a little more moisture in the atmosphere... wait, moisture? That's right! There was a rain technique!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: That's not it! I have a proper plan!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: ...Well, whatever. I've had about enough of your little show.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: It's time I close the curtains...!

dialogue.speaker: Alabasta rebel
dialogue.text: I'll make a 'beehive out of your body!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: As a way of thanking you for your per- formance,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Allow me to show you something interesting too.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I have to do it now!

summary: This part develops steel can be cut and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
